payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields should capture enough information to reproduce usage, cost, and performance metrics. Token counts should include input/prompt tokens, output/completion tokens, total tokens, and any cached, reasoning, or tool-call tokens if the provider reports them. Provider and model IDs should record the exact provider name and model identifier used for the run, including version or snapshot when available. Estimated price should be calculated from the recorded token counts and the applicable provider pricing schedule, usually separated into input cost, output cost, and total estimated cost; it should be labeled as an estimate if final billing data is unavailable. Latency metrics should measure elapsed time for requests and summarize the distribution across runs: p50 is the median latency, p90 is the latency below which 90% of requests completed, and p95 is the latency below which 95% of requests completed. These should specify units, usually milliseconds, and clarify whether they measure end-to-end latency, model-only latency, time to first token, or full completion latency.
payload.decision: No durable memory should be committed because the input is an instruction/request about eval accounting, not a stable user fact or preference.

entity_resolution.reason: No persistent entity or user-specific fact needs to be created, updated, or resolved.

payload.intent: Explain the required evaluation accounting fields for cost, latency, and token tracking.

details: Returned a single JSON object matching the requested schema.
Did not create durable memory for non-user-specific accounting guidance.
Preserved accounting ambiguity by describing estimated pricing and latency scope requirements.
